skill.name: square-automation
skill.description: Guides Square payment, order, invoice, and location workflows through Rube MCP using current Square tool schemas.
skill.summary: Automate Square payment, order, invoice, and location tasks through Rube MCP.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static external-command, weak-crypto, and reconnaissance alerts are false positives caused by markdown backticks, Square tool names, and ordinary field names. The real risk is operational: the skill uses an external Rube MCP endpoint and documents Square actions that can cancel payments or invoices and update orders.

medium_findings.title: Operational Access to Square Business Records
medium_findings.description: The skill documents workflows that can cancel pending payments, update orders, and cancel invoices through Square tools. This is legitimate automation, but it can affect financial and order records if the user authorizes the wrong action or skips confirmation.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced workflows explicitly include cancel and update operations for Square resources. The intent is legitimate, but the business impact is clear enough to require a marketplace warning.

medium_findings.title: External MCP Service Dependency
medium_findings.description: The setup instructions require adding the Rube MCP endpoint. This is expected for the skill, but it sends Square workflow requests through a third-party MCP service and requires users to manage OAuth access carefully.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file directly instructs users to connect Rube MCP and authenticate Square. No malicious exfiltration is shown, but the external dependency is explicit.

low_findings.title: Static External Command Alerts Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged many markdown backticks as shell execution. The file contains documentation for MCP tool names and Square parameters, not Ruby backtick execution or shell commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review shows markdown-formatted tool names, parameters, and examples only. There is no script body, shell command, or executable Ruby context in SKILL.md.

low_findings.title: Static Weak Crypto Alerts Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography alerts correspond to ordinary Square documentation text, such as sorting terms and response field names. No hashing, encryption, or cryptographic API use appears in the skill file.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are descriptive markdown and Square field documentation. I found no evidence of MD5, DES, SHA1, or other weak cryptographic implementation.

low_findings.title: Static Reconnaissance Alerts Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The reconnaissance alerts map to resource identifiers, status fields, and quick-reference rows for Square operations. They do not instruct the model to inspect the host system or collect local environment details.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The context is Square object lookup and workflow documentation. I found no host reconnaissance commands, file-system probing, or local network enumeration instructions.

content.value_statement: Square operations often require careful tool selection, IDs, versions, and pagination. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through repeatable Square workflows using Rube MCP.
actual_capabilities: Guides setup of Rube MCP and Square OAuth connection checks.
Lists and filters Square payments by time range, location, cursor, and sort order.
Searches, retrieves, and updates Square orders with location IDs and version handling.
Lists Square locations and resolves location IDs for later workflows.
Lists, retrieves, and cancels eligible Square invoices using current invoice versions.
Documents pagination, RFC 3339 dates, response parsing, and rate-limit handling.
limitations: Requires an active Rube MCP server and an authorized Square connection.
Depends on live Square tool schemas returned by RUBE_SEARCH_TOOLS.
Does not cover refunds, customer management, catalog updates, or subscriptions.
Financial changes still require user review and proper Square permissions.

use_cases.title: Review Daily Payments
use_cases.description: List payments for a date range, filter by location, and follow pagination until all results are reviewed.

use_cases.title: Find and Update Orders
use_cases.description: Resolve location IDs, search orders by status or date, retrieve details, and update records with version checks.

use_cases.title: Manage Open Invoices
use_cases.description: List invoices for a Square location, inspect invoice details, and cancel eligible unpaid invoices after confirmation.

prompt_templates.title: Check Square Connection
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the Square automation skill. Search Rube tools first, then check whether my Square connection is active. Do not run any business action yet.

prompt_templates.title: List Recent Payments
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the Square automation skill to list payments for the last seven days. Ask me for the location if needed, and include pagination until complete.

prompt_templates.title: Search Orders by Status
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the Square automation skill to find open orders for my selected location. Retrieve full details for matching orders and summarize the next actions.

prompt_templates.title: Cancel Eligible Invoice Safely
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the Square automation skill to find an unpaid invoice by location and invoice ID. Retrieve the current version, explain the impact, and ask for confirmation before cancellation.

output_examples.input: List payments from yesterday for the downtown location.
output: Confirms the Square connection and searches current tool schemas.
Resolves the downtown location ID before listing payments.
Returns a concise payment summary with date range, status, amount, and pagination notes.

output_examples.input: Find unpaid invoices for my main location and cancel one after I approve.
output: Lists locations and selects the requested location.
Lists matching invoices and retrieves the selected invoice details.
Requests confirmation before using the invoice version to cancel the invoice.

output_examples.input: Search for open orders this week and prepare updates.
output: Uses location IDs and date filters to search orders.
Retrieves full details for selected orders.
Identifies required version values before any update operation.

best_practices: Search Rube tools first because Square schemas can change.
Confirm IDs, versions, and business impact before updates or cancellations.
Use pagination and rate-limit backoff for large payment or order lists.
anti_patterns: Do not cancel payments, invoices, or orders without explicit user confirmation.
Do not reuse cached location IDs when the business account may have changed.
Do not assume money fields are decimal amounts; Square often returns cents.

faq.question: Does this skill require a Square API key?
faq.answer: No API key is listed in the skill. It requires Rube MCP and a Square OAuth connection.

faq.question: Can it cancel completed payments?
faq.answer: No. The skill states that only pending payments can be cancelled. Completed payments require refunds, which are not covered.

faq.question: Why does it search tools before each workflow?
faq.answer: The skill tells the assistant to fetch current Rube tool schemas before calling Square tools.

faq.question: Can it update Square orders?
faq.answer: Yes. It documents order updates, including the need for the current version to avoid conflicts.

faq.question: What should I confirm before invoice cancellation?
faq.answer: Confirm the location, invoice ID, invoice status, current version, and business impact before cancellation.

faq.question: Is this skill safe to publish?
faq.answer: It is publishable with a warning. The main risk is authorized financial operations through an external MCP service.
